current_public_release.title: Product Proof Path exact terminal-chain refusal groups

website.verification_hint: Compare the live file with release.json on the website repository main branch. The file does not embed its own website commit.

authority_boundary: A log records what happened. A ZLAR receipt records what counted as authorized effect when action tried to become consequence.

claim_boundary.summary: Product Proof Path exact terminal-chain refusal group preservation over terminal_chain_boundary, while preserving Product Proof Path terminal-chain artifact verification, Product Proof Path artifact binding over supplied proof-pack artifact/verification pairs, release-forward contract parser repair, release-forward report contract manifest evidence, Product Proof Path binding, release-forward registry refusal summary preservation, registry refusal summary surfacing, terminal-chain registry refusal boundary, terminal-chain registry recognition summary evidence, product proof registry recognition component evidence, release-forward registry recognition evidence boundary, private result verification evidence, issuer-status evidence reviewability, explicit v3.4.31 release-asset byte-binding evidence, verifier-owned nested binding summary, nested artifact tamper-refusal evidence, readiness observed-summary contracts, terminal-chain recognition-refusal group case-ID evidence, recognition-refusal group evidence, named receipt-refusal evidence, verifier-kit diagnostics boundary, and recognition-contract digest boundary.
claim_boundary.evidence_threshold: v3.4.46 release checks pass with Product Proof Path exact terminal-chain refusal groups: Product Proof Path now preserves the exact terminal-chain recognition-refusal group contract in its terminal-chain boundary instead of compressing already-existing grouped refusal evidence into hash-only proof. The Product Proof Path, North Star readiness, and v3.4.46+ release-forward report contract now require recognition_refusal_group_count=3, recognition_refusal_group_case_count=18, exact recognition_refusal_group_case_ids, and recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_preserved=true. Remote CI, CodeQL, and OpenSSF Scorecard are green for commit 372936af3343ebde229e2586b484da2fe5d1cb42. This is local Product Proof Path and release-forward contract hardening over generated local terminal-chain evidence only: no new verifier result, no non-operator review, no public external attestation, no live registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no current-machine governance, no live MCP coverage, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no enterprise readiness, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Current release title: Product Proof Path exact terminal-chain refusal groups. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.45 release checks pass with Product Proof Path terminal-chain boundary: the Product Proof Path now generates and verifies a fresh terminal-chain artifact and carries the terminal-chain artifact-verification boundary into Product Proof Path, North Star readiness, and the v3.4.45+ release-forward report contract. The boundary preserves public-safe hash/refusal fields, terminal-chain artifact verification, trusted-issuer registry recognition binding/refusal hashes, exact registry refusal case IDs and reason codes, raw public-key omission, receipt-envelope omission, and false current-machine governance/public-attestation flags. Remote CI, CodeQL, and OpenSSF Scorecard are green for commit f5a50daf5fc9d8daa93e77c4b2fd17d9c012b754. This is local Product Proof Path and release-forward contract hardening over generated local terminal-chain evidence only: no new verifier result, no non-operator review, no public external attestation, no live registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no current-machine governance, no live MCP coverage, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no enterprise readiness, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Current release title: Product Proof Path terminal-chain boundary. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.44 release checks pass with Product Proof Path artifact binding: the Product Proof Path builder recomputes local proof-pack artifact verification before accepting any supplied artifact/verification pair, so stale verification JSON cannot be paired with a mutated proof-pack artifact carrying authority-shaped fields such as external_attestation=true. Remote CI, CodeQL, and OpenSSF Scorecard are green for commit ca22239373f1fef56c1f60267c956ee3599f9d13. This is local Product Proof Path evidence-binding hardening only: no new verifier result, no non-operator review, no public external attestation, no live registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no current-machine governance, no live MCP coverage, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no enterprise readiness, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Current release title: Product Proof Path artifact binding. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.43 release checks pass with Release-forward contract parser repair: the v3.4.42+ Product Proof Path contract binding now runs through the release-forward dry-run helper under Node 22 in both local verification and GitHub Actions. Remote CI, CodeQL, and OpenSSF Scorecard are green for commit 88a788b042d48c5df62bc7ea12b513502394f560. The v3.4.42+ contract binds ZLAR/zlar-product-proof-path-v1.json into release_forward_report_contract with source path/SHA-256, PASS/evidence-model fields, false forbidden claims, simulated human authorization behavior, receipt-verifier boundary, North Star consumption, and known unrouted-records noncoverage visibility. This is release-forward packet contract hardening and executable-path repair only: no new verifier result, no non-operator review, no public external attestation, no live registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no current-machine governance, no live MCP coverage, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no enterprise readiness, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.42 release checks pass with Product Proof Path contract binding: release-forward verifier packets bind the Product Proof Path artifact into release_forward_report_contract for explicit v3.4.42+ targets while keeping live probing false and all stronger-claim flags false. v3.4.41 release checks pass with release-forward report contract manifest: release-forward verifier packets now preserve release_forward_report_contract in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json for explicit v3.4.41+ targets and render the canonical manifest object into DRY-RUN-RESULT.md as manifest.release_forward_report_contract.* lines for human review. The object is a compact machine-readable summary of the existing release-forward dry-run report contract, derived only from preserved local dry-run artifacts and same-manifest terminal-chain refusal evidence. It records contract_type=zlar-release-forward-dry-run-report-contract-v1, evidence_model=release-forward-dry-run-artifacts, minimum_target=v3.4.41, report_contract_satisfied=true, manifest_is_canonical=true, source artifact SHA-256s, exact terminal-chain recognition-refusal group counts and case IDs, exact trusted-issuer registry refusal case IDs and reason codes, matching trusted-registry refusal hashes, Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed-summary preservation, and false claim-boundary flags. Regression coverage proves v3.4.40 targets omit release_forward_report_contract while v3.4.41+ targets require and preserve it. This is verifier packet contract hardening only: no new verifier result, no non-operator review, no public external attestation, no live registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no current-machine governance, no live MCP coverage, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no enterprise readiness, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_enterprise_readiness=false, proves_sovereign_recognition=false, and proves_unrouted_surface_coverage=false. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.40 release checks pass with release-forward registry refusal summaries: release-forward verifier packets now preserve the v3.4.39 terminal-chain trusted-issuer registry refusal summary in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and print it in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md for explicit v3.4.39+ targets. The preserved release-forward terminal_chain_refusal_evidence includes case_count=2, exact case IDs (unrecognized_terminal_chain_registry_scope_refused, registry_receipt_contract_mismatch_refused), exact reason codes (scope_not_found, detail_hash_mismatch), all_refused=true, matching terminal-chain/artifact-verification trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_sha256, and all_trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_preserved=true. DRY-RUN-RESULT.md also mirrors the proof-smoke and North Star readiness Release-Forward Report Contract fields, including Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed-summary preservation. Regression coverage proves v3.4.38 targets omit these release-forward summary fields while v3.4.39+ targets require and preserve them. This is release-forward reporting hardening over already-bounded local fixture refusal evidence only: no live trust-registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no production trust registry, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no current-machine governance, no enterprise readiness, no public external attestation, no real non-operator review, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_production_trust_registry=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, and proves_sovereign_recognition=false. Previous release evidence remains preserved for audit context: v3.4.39 release checks pass with registry refusal summary surfacing: proof-smoke and North Star readiness now expose terminal-chain-local trusted-issuer registry refusal evidence as first-class evaluator summary fields: case_count=2, exact case IDs (unrecognized_terminal_chain_registry_scope_refused, registry_receipt_contract_mismatch_refused), exact reason codes (scope_not_found, detail_hash_mismatch), all_refused=true, and trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_sha256 for both terminal-chain evidence and terminal-chain artifact verification; North Star readiness mirrors the same fields into Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries. This is summary surfacing of already-bounded local fixture refusal evidence only: no live trust-registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no production trust registry, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no current-machine governance, no enterprise readiness, no public external attestation, no real non-operator review, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage. Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_production_trust_registry=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, and proves_sovereign_recognition=false. The previous v3.4.38 release preserved terminal-chain-local trusted-issuer registry refusal evidence inside the hash-bound trusted issuer registry recognition binding: unrecognized registry scope refuses with scope_not_found; a valid signed receipt whose registry-bound receipt contract mismatches refuses with detail_hash_mismatch while preserving signature_valid=true and issuer_status=active; artifact verification and CLI preserve case_count=2, all_refused=true, exact case IDs/reasons, trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_sha256, registry_receipt_contract_hash_bound=true, and the full false-boundary field contract. This remains bundled-local-fixture terminal-chain evidence only: no live trust-registry state, no live issuer status, no key custody, no revocation truth, no production trust registry, no production downstream recognition, no production authority, no current-machine governance, no enterprise readiness, no public external attestation, no real non-operator review, no sovereign recognition, and no unrouted-surface coverage.  Explicit false proof flags remain preserved: proves_live_registry=false, proves_live_issuer_status=false, proves_key_custody=false, proves_revocation_truth=false, proves_production_trust_registry=false, proves_production_downstream_recognition=false, proves_production_authority=false, proves_current_machine_governance=false, proves_public_external_attestation=false, proves_real_non_operator_review=false, and proves_sovereign_recognition=false. The previous v3.4.37 release bound terminal-chain trusted-issuer registry recognition to the selected installed-runtime profile, preflight, service proof, service-proof artifact verification, terminal-chain artifact, and terminal-chain artifact verification: v3.4.37 local terminal-chain, proof-smoke, and North Star readiness evidence preserve terminal_chain_trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_binding / trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_binding as a public-safe hash-bound registry/receipt summary with registry_contract_sha256, receipt_contract_sha256, registry_receipt_contract_hash_bound=true, registry_public_key_material_included=false, receipt_envelope_included=false, and cryptographic_evidence_reproducible_from_artifact=false; the fixture registry evaluates to RECOGNIZED, malformed registries fail closed before verdict, proof-smoke and North Star readiness preserve the full terminal-chain registry non-claim flags in Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries, and false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, current-machine-governance, public-attestation, real-non-operator-review, and sovereign-recognition claim flags remain false. The previous v3.4.36 release made the Product Proof Path trusted-issuer registry recognition component first-class while preserving release-forward trusted-issuer registry recognition evidence, private result verification evidence, issuer-status fixture evidence, release-asset readiness handoff, and verifier-owned terminal-chain nested binding evidence: v3.4.36 local proof-pack and Product Proof Path reports include trusted_issuer_registry_recognition as a first-class component, validate and evaluate a valid bundled trusted-receipt-issuers-v1 registry fixture through the shared downstream recognition rule, and record registry_fixture_validated=true, registry_fixture_evaluated=true, registry_to_recognition_rule_evaluated=true, registry_evaluation_result_type=downstream-recognition-rule-v1, registry_trusted_issuer_count=1, required audit-event/detail-hash binding, issuer_status=active, signature_valid=true, and malformed_registry_fail_closed_before_verdict=true; proof-smoke and North Star readiness consume that component instead of reconstructing registry recognition from adjacent summaries; false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, current-machine-governance, public-attestation, real-non-operator-review, and sovereign-recognition claim flags remain false. The previous v3.4.35 release made release-forward trusted-issuer registry recognition evidence first-class: v3.4.35 release-forward verifier packets include trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_evidence in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and a Trusted Issuer Registry Recognition Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, binding the supplied recognition JSON, malformed-registry JSON, and malformed-registry error hashes; recording registry_type=trusted-receipt-issuers-v1, registry_evidence_model=bundled-local-fixture, live_probing=false, verdict=RECOGNIZED, issuer_status=active, signature_valid=true, and malformed_registry_fail_closed_before_verdict=true; and preserving false live-registry, live-issuer-status, key-custody, revocation-truth, production-trust-registry, production-downstream-recognition, production-authority, sovereign-recognition, public-attestation, public-attribution, and non-operator-review claim flags. The previous v3.4.34 release made private result verification evidence first-class: v3.4.34 release-forward verifier packets include a Private Result Verification Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, preserving private-result verification verdict, recomputed evidence-dir hash status, artifact count, sample/non-operator boundary, private_non_operator_pass_validated=false for generated sample evidence, and false public-attestation/public-attribution/public-non-operator-review claim flags. This is bundled-local-fixture and terminal-chain registry recognition reviewability over generated local/release-forward evidence. It does not prove live registry state, live issuer status, key custody, revocation truth, production trust registry, production downstream recognition, production authority, sovereign recognition, public external attestation, real non-operator review, or enterprise readiness. v3.4.33 continues to provide issuer-status evidence reviewability: release-forward verifier packets include issuer_status_evidence in DRY-RUN-MANIFEST.json and an Issuer Status Evidence section in DRY-RUN-RESULT.md, preserving the local hermetic issuer-status proof, verifier-kit issuer fixture verdict, active-issuer boarding, retired/compromised/missing-status/unknown/missing-key issuer refusals, no-live boundary, and false live/custody/revocation/production-registry/downstream claim flags. v3.4.32 continues the release-asset readiness handoff without widening asset claims: release-forward verifier dry runs use live explicit-tag GitHub release-asset reads plus --require-public public-distribution validation only for the explicit asset-bearing v3.4.31 public ZLAR-AI/ZLAR verifier-kit target; later proof-hardening releases do not inherit a public asset claim unless that exact release publishes the required assets, and North Star readiness rebuilds public-distribution posture from raw release-asset, reproducibility, and local dist-file evidence before accepting a public-ready claim. v3.4.31 remains the uploaded byte-bound verifier-kit asset evidence: live public release assets for the verifier kit are published, zlar verifier-kit-release-assets-live-read emits zlar-verifier-kit-release-assets-live-v1 with all required assets present and downloaded, and zlar verifier-kit-public-distribution --require-public reports public_distribution_posture_ready with ready_for_public_distribution_claim=true. v3.4.30 proof-smoke, North Star readiness, and release-forward evidence preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_binding as a verifier-owned artifact-verification summary with nested artifact body hashes, verified flags, binding booleans, and false stronger-claim flags. v3.4.28+ terminal-chain reports and portable artifacts embed nested generated preflight and service-proof artifacts, and release-forward dry-run exports terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals with forged_inner_preflight_hash_refused=true and forged_inner_service_hash_refused=true after refusing forged inner preflight and service-proof hashes while recomputing outer terminal-chain artifact integrity. Prior readiness evidence remains preserved: v3.4.25+ proof-smoke and North Star readiness preserve group_count=3, case_count=18, and exact grouped case IDs for both terminal-chain evidence and terminal-chain artifact verification. For v3.4.26+ the Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries mirror that same case-ID contract. v3.4.24+ release-forward verifier packets still preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_required=true, and all_recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_preserved=true for no usable recognized receipt authority, recognized receipt scope mismatch, and route/request authority material refusal groups. v3.4.23+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve recognition_refusal_groups_sha256 and grouped before-mutation booleans. v3.4.22+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve named_receipt_refusals_sha256 and named refusal booleans for missing, invalid, stale, unknown-issuer, wrong-policy, wrong-domain, and wrong-tool receipt cases. v3.4.21+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve zlar-verifier-kit-external-runner-diagnostics-v1.json, and v3.4.19+ readiness, proof-smoke, and release-forward dry-run checks still preserve installed-runtime-profile recognition-contract SHA-256 binding. Previous v3.4.43 release title: Release-forward contract parser repair. Manifest field inventory preserved for verifier tooling: zlar-trusted-receipt-issuer-recognition-malformed-registry.json, zlar-trusted-receipt-issuer-recognition-malformed-registry-error.txt, nested_artifacts, terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals.forged_inner_preflight_hash_refused, terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_tamper_refusals.forged_inner_service_hash_refused, v3.4.26+ North Star readiness observed summaries mirror the terminal-chain case-ID contract, installed_runtime_profile_recognition_contract_sha256, and all_trusted_issuer_registry_recognition_refusals_preserved=true. This remains local disposable installed-runtime-profile recognition-refusal group case-id, recognition-refusal group, and named-refusal evidence only.
claim_boundary.safe_codex_wording: ZLAR can govern Codex CLI-invoked MCP tool calls when those MCP servers are routed through ZLAR.
non_claims: No hook, user, or machine configuration claim.
No raw public key material in the public-safe terminal-chain artifact.
No receipt envelope in the public-safe terminal-chain artifact.
No reproducible cryptographic evidence from the public-safe terminal-chain artifact alone.
